page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX, AVX-512, and AES instructions. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options.

Benchmark results place the D8s_v4 consistently in the average tier for general compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1155.0 and a multi-core score of 4610.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 414.43 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 44.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. While database operations and Redis SET performance at 6.84 million operations per second remain average, certain workloads like bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM prompt processing exhibit weaker relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8s_v4 offers a balanced resource density for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage. The lack of local NVMe SSDs represents a tradeoff, requiring users to provision external Premium Storage for disk-heavy operations. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Benchmark performance is generally average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4610.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 6.84 million operations per second, though bzip3 decompression and LLM prompt processing show weaker results. This server is optimized for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and moderate database tasks where local storage is not a prerequisite.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for balanced workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v4 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine with 32 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and flexible remote storage options for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per vCPU)
Supports Premium Storage with no local disk allocation
Delivers average performance across general compute and database benchmarks
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications
